evidence.reason: The official mod build defines the Arcane Armor Fabricator as a naturally available pre-boss crafting station.
Soul Forge recipes require Hallowed Bars and all three mechanical-boss souls, so the station becomes available after all mechanical bosses.
Both Borean Strider forms and its treasure bag belong to the same boss encounter.
All three Forgotten One forms and its treasure bag are loot sources for the same boss encounter.
These are ordinary pre-Hardmode enemies, critters, fishing crates, or naturally generated containers with no boss progression requirement.
The profile places the first Goblin Army availability after Eye of Cthulhu.
Dungeon access and Dungeon lock boxes are gated by Skeletron; the listed Thorium enemies use the normal Dungeon spawn pool.
Patch Werk and its associated Blood Moon enemies are pre-Hardmode Blood Moon content; the profile starts Blood Moon progression at Eye of Cthulhu.
The official build permits Corpse Bloom after the world-evil boss and permits the Druid to move in after Corpse Bloom is defeated.
These enemies, mimics, and crate variants require Hardmode but no later boss flag.
The listed Dungeon and Temple enemies become reachable in the post-Plantera portion of progression.
These sources belong to post-Plantera Frost Moon or equivalent blizzard content.
Martian probes and Martian Madness become available after Golem.
The Cobbler can arrive as soon as a player owns qualifying footwear.
The official build's CanTownNPCSpawn requires King Slime to be defeated.
The official build's CanTownNPCSpawn requires The Grand Thunder Bird to be defeated.
The official build requires Eye of Cthulhu for both the Tracker and Blacksmith to move in.
The Confused Zombie arrives after Patch Werk, a pre-Hardmode Blood Moon miniboss placed at the Eye of Cthulhu stage.
The official build's CanTownNPCSpawn requires Buried Champion to be defeated.
The official build's CanTownNPCSpawn requires Coznix, the Fallen Beholder, to be defeated.
These vanilla vendors have no boss requirement.
The Mechanic is rescued in the Dungeon and the Clothier arrives after Skeletron.
The Witch Doctor arrives after Queen Bee.
These vendors require Hardmode.
The Cyborg arrives after Plantera.
Server configuration toggles enable or disable content but do not represent a progression milestone; ingredients and vendor stages remain authoritative.
The official mod build obtains these materials from world-generation tiles, pre-Hardmode biome enemies, plants, or pre-Hardmode fishing without a boss flag.
The official drop rules obtain these materials during the early Blood Moon or from ordinary early Desert enemies; the profile begins those event drops at Eye of Cthulhu.
Hallowed Charms are crafted from Pixie Dust and Souls of Light, making them available at the start of Hardmode.
The official build generates Illumite when Plantera dies, while Holy Knight's Alloy drops from the post-Plantera Paladin.

The official build supplies these through starting items, world generation, breakable plants, pre-Hardmode fishing, ordinary biome enemies, or pre-boss chests; these custom hooks are not represented by the standard tModLoader recipe/drop export.
The official SetupTravelShop hook adds these items only after the world-evil boss; the legacy travel-shop hook is not included in the snapshot shop export.
The official Confused Zombie shop code sells the five repellents after Patch Werk unlocks the vendor.
These are right-click conversion variants of the craftable melee Thor's Hammer and share its pre-Hardmode availability.
All ingredients and stations for these recipes are available before the first required boss; custom tile, fishing, or world-generation ingredients are confirmed by the official build.
These recipes require Dungeon books, Valor, Grave Goods, Ancient Aegis, or equivalent post-Skeletron ingredients.
Spore Coating requires the Imbuing Station, which becomes available from the Witch Doctor after Queen Bee.
These recipes require a Hardmode anvil, Crystal Shards, Souls, hardmode enemy drops, or equivalent early-Hardmode ingredients.
The official shop code sells the inactive music player after Star Scouter, which is the key ingredient for this variant.
The recipe requires Hallowed Bars and Cogs, so it is available after all mechanical bosses in this profile order.
Both recipes require Shroomite Bars, which are post-Plantera.
Terrarium Defender requires Terrarium Cores and the Lunar Crafting Station, placing it after Moon Lord.
The Nurse and Aquatic Depths Crown of Thorns are available without a boss requirement.
Goblin Trapper spawns in the Goblin Army, which this profile opens at Eye of Cthulhu.
Sea Shanty Singer spawns in the Hardmode Pirate Invasion.
The official SpawnChance requires all three mechanical bosses and a Solar Eclipse.
A pre-Hardmode restriction does not require a defeated boss.
Hardmode begins after Wall of Flesh.
The NeverTrue branches are tModLoader's hidden UI representation of the boss's normal-mode loot; they belong to the Borean Strider victory stage.
The NeverTrue branches are tModLoader's hidden UI representation of the boss's normal-mode loot; they belong to the Forgotten One victory stage.
Ordinary Mimics are Hardmode enemies.
This hidden recipe only describes reverse Shimmer decrafting; Lasting Pliers already has its real Cobbler drop source, so the dummy condition must not create a later progression gate.
These are environment, difficulty, bestiary, world-generation fallback, or player-stat restrictions rather than boss progression; the vendor's own stage remains authoritative.
Cook recipes are unlocked by supplying ingredients after the Cook arrives; this custom counter is not a later boss milestone.
Tracker contracts are side objectives available through the Tracker after Eye of Cthulhu, not separate boss stages.
Thrombosis is intentionally a melee weapon with a summon-class effect, so both detected class affinities are retained.

evidence.sourceVersion: Recommendation snapshot labels for Thorium Mod 1.7.2.5
evidence.reason: These identifiers are recommendation-only group or legacy labels, not exact item IDs in the current tModLoader snapshot; recommendations must not alter availability.

The official build marks these as removed, development-only, hidden state variants, non-inventory Bard pickups, or unobtainable items with no normal-world acquisition path; they are not player-selectable combat equipment.
The current official snapshot exposes no recipe, drop, shop, fishing, or world-generation acquisition path for this optional donator item.
Assassin armor is a recommendation group label rather than an exact current item ID; it must not affect availability.
Rhapsodist armor is a recommendation group label rather than an exact current item ID; it must not affect availability.
